findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 33. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The PDF uses a two-column time/content layout (time on left, agenda items on right) which is collapsed into a single-column format in the HTML, losing the visual association between times and their corresponding agenda items.
findings.evidence: PDF shows '9:00 a.m.' in a left column aligned with 'Meeting Overview – Lara Klibansky' in a right column. HTML renders these as separate lines with bold time labels inline.

findings.description: Sub-items under Chairman's Report are rendered differently. In the PDF, items like Letters, Ethics Training, etc. are indented sub-items without bullet markers, while the HTML renders them as bulleted list items.
findings.evidence: PDF shows indented text without bullets under Chairman's Report. HTML shows bullet points (•) for Letters, Ethics Training, 2020 Meeting Schedule, etc.

findings.description: The PDF uses a two-column time/content layout for the agenda which is collapsed into a single-column format in the HTML on page 2.
findings.evidence: PDF shows times like '11:15 a.m.', '12:15 p.m.', '1:15 p.m.' etc. in a left column with content in a right column. HTML renders them inline.

findings.description: The document has nested <main> elements - one in the body wrapper and another inside the content area, which is invalid HTML structure but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <body><main>...<main>...</main>...</main></body> with two nested main elements.

findings.description: Chairman's Report sub-items are rendered as a bulleted list in HTML, whereas in the PDF they are indented sub-items without bullet markers. This is a minor formatting difference that does not change meaning.
findings.evidence: PDF shows indented items (Letters, Ethics Training..., 2020 Meeting Schedule, Commission Committee Assignments, Committee Reports) under Chairman's Report. HTML uses <ul><li> for these items.
